title: overscroll-behavior
description: Controls what happens when scrolling reaches the edge of a container. overscroll-behavior: contain stops the scroll from chaining to the parent — content behind a scrollable panel stays put. Baseline widely available since 2022.
html:
↓ Scroll the purple panel past its last item to see scroll chaining

Item 1
Item 2
Item 3
Item 4
Item 5
Item 6
Item 7 — scroll past here

⚠️ You reached the page below the panel — scroll chaining happened!

Add overscroll-behavior: contain to .panel to prevent this.
challenge: Scroll the purple panel to Item 7 and keep scrolling — the outer page scrolls and reveals the warning section below. Add overscroll-behavior: contain to .panel so the outer page stays fixed when the panel reaches its end.
realWorldUsage: Modals and side sheets on mobile where scrolling the modal content shouldn't scroll the page behind it, chat windows and comment threads embedded in a larger scrollable layout, and any fixed-height panel inside a scrolling page. Previously required JavaScript touch event hacks; now it's one CSS property.
